page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c5.2xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8163 processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 8 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing 8 vCPUs. The instance is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or bundled GPU accelerators, and it operates on a KVM hypervisor with a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates that the processor delivers weak single-core and multi-core performance, placing in the bottom 25% for stress-ng and Geekbench tests. Memory bandwidth for small cached blocks is also weak, though large block read and write operations achieve average tier performance. Extended CPU instructions, encryption, and small-model LLM inference (135M model prompt processing and text generation) perform in the average range. However, tasks such as single-threaded gzip compression and text generation on larger 7B models fall into the poor category.

The instance offers dedicated CPU allocation, which ensures consistent compute resources without virtualization-induced resource sharing. However, the lack of local storage requires external block storage attachments, and the 2 Gbps network baseline may limit high-throughput network applications. Given the 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and average performance in basic encryption and instruction sets, this instance is suited for compute-focused workloads with moderate memory requirements, such as basic web serving, lightweight batch processing, and utility computing, rather than memory-intensive databases or large-scale LLM inference.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c5.2xlarge is a compute-optimized x86_64 server instance featuring 8 dedicated Intel Xeon 8163 vCPUs running at 2.5 GHz and 16.0 GB of RAM. Operating on a KVM hypervisor, it provides a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Performance benchmarks show weak single-core and multi-core CPU capabilities, alongside weak memory bandwidth for small cached blocks. However, it achieves average performance in large-block memory operations, CPU encryption, and small-model LLM inference. With a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated CPU allocation, this instance is suitable for moderate compute workloads, basic web serving, and lightweight batch processing where high memory density is not required.
og_description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c5.2xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 16.0 GB RAM, and 2 Gbps network bandwidth, suited for moderate compute and batch processing workloads.

tagline: An x86_64 compute-optimized instance featuring eight dedicated Intel Xeon cores, sixteen gigabytes of RAM, and a two gigabit baseline network.

KVM hypervisor with 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth
No local storage or GPU accelerators included
